message.content: client access 日志记录的传输类型不正确。当前用全局布尔 g_isThroughUb 推断 UB/SHM，无法表达 TCP 回退；而且 Record 在 publish/get 路径上分散调用，状态和真实传输不一致。请引入按线程的 AccessTransportTracker（SHM/UB/TCP），在请求入口 Reset、在传输决策点由调用方 Record 最终结果，并补单测。另外修复并行 MSet URMA 失败回退 TCP 时本地 buffer 布局错位（payload 起始地址偏移）的问题。

content.thinking: Let me make sure I understand the requirement. Today access logging infers the transport from a global boolean g_isThroughUb — that is binary (UB vs SHM) and cannot represent the TCP fallback path, and a global is wrong for a per-request, per-thread property. I need a thread-local AccessTransportTracker with three states (SHM/UB/TCP), Reset at request entry, and Record called by the caller at each transport decision. I also need to audit every place Record/g_isThroughUb is touched so the final transport is recorded exactly once at the right point. Separately there is a parallel-MSet bug: on URMA write failure we fall back to a TCP payload publish, but the local fallback buffer is sized for the data only while the rest of the code assumes a metadata+data layout, so the payload start is offset. Let me explore the logging and buffer code first.

content.text: I'll start by reading the access recorder and tracing how the transport type is currently inferred and recorded.
Reading the buffer publish path where the UB-vs-SHM-vs-TCP decision is actually made.

content.thinking: Now I can see the three root causes clearly. (1) g_isThroughUb only encodes UB vs SHM; the TCP fallback in Buffer::Publish is invisible, so a fallback is logged as whatever the boolean last held. (2) Record placement is wrong: Buffer::Publish’s UB-success path never records at the caller, so the tracker stays at the Reset value (kShm); and GetBuffersFromWorker calls Record(kUb) BEFORE ValidateMeta, so a meta failure or count-mismatch TCP fallback leaves the tracker stuck on kUb. (3) The MSet fallback buffer is allocated as dataSize+1 but the reader/writer use a pointer+metadataSize layout, and AppendPublishPayload copies dataSize bytes from pointer rather than pointer+metadataSize — so the payload start is offset and the first bytes can read back as \0. Fix: a thread_local AccessTransportTracker {kShm,kUb,kTcp}, Reset at request entry, Record by the caller at each decision point; remove the internal Record from SendBufferViaUb; allocate metadataSize+dataSize; offset the AppendPublishPayload copy by metadataSize.

todos.content: Replace g_isThroughUb in buffer.cpp; Record(UB) on success, Record(TCP) on fallback
Move Record to callers in object_client_impl (after meta validation)
Fix MSet TCP-fallback buffer layout (metadataSize+dataSize + AppendPublishPayload offset)
Add access_transport_tracker_test unit tests + extend urma_object_client_test
Build and run the affected tests

content.text: Adding the thread-local tracker to the access recorder.
Fixing the MSet fallback allocation and payload offset, and moving Record after meta validation.

input.old_string:
recorder.Record(kUb);            // recorded BEFORE meta validation
  RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(ValidateMeta(...));   // if this fails or count mismatches we fall back to TCP
  ...
  AppendPublishPayload(pointer, dataSize);  // reads dataSize from pointer, not pointer+metadataSize

content.text: Building and running the new unit test and the object-client storage test.

input.description: Run the URMA object-client storage test (covers MSet TCP fallback)

content.text:
Done. Summary of the fix:
- Replaced the global g_isThroughUb with a thread-local AccessTransportTracker (kShm/kUb/kTcp); Reset at request entry.
- Record is now done by the caller at each transport decision: Buffer::Publish records kUb on success / kTcp on fallback; SendBufferViaUb only sends; GetBuffersFromWorker records kUb only after meta validation and kTcp on the count-mismatch TCP fallback.
- Fixed the parallel-MSet TCP fallback buffer layout: allocate metadataSize+dataSize and offset AppendPublishPayload by metadataSize so the payload start is correct (no leading \0).
- Added access_transport_tracker_test (4 cases) and extended urma_object_client_test. All tests pass.
